model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: This Elite Eight matchup is a highly anticipated rematch of the 2025 National Championship game, featuring two of the nation's most elite defensive units. While Houston boasts a top-tier adjusted defense and a physically imposing style, Florida's slightly more efficient offense and experience as the defending champions provide them with a narrow edge. The Gators' ability to maintain composure in high-leverage situations, combined with their top-10 offensive ranking, should allow them to navigate Houston's pressure and secure a return trip to the Final Four.

reasoningStep.title: South Regional Final Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Florida and Houston are nearly identical in advanced metrics, but Florida's offensive efficiency and championship pedigree give them the advantage in this rematch of the 2025 title game.
evidence: Florida ranks #4 in KenPom with the #9 adjusted offense and #5 adjusted defense.
Houston ranks #5 in KenPom with the #16 adjusted offense and #4 adjusted defense.
Florida defeated Houston in the 2025 National Championship game.
Both teams entered the tournament healthy and have maintained their top-5 analytical profiles through the first three rounds.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Key March Madness injuries to know while filling out your men’s NCAA Tournament bracket - The Athletic Man City vs. Real Madrid Reactions Arsenal vs. Leverkusen Reactions Dowman for England? Chelsea’s PL Punishment Most Underrated Footballers Premier League Champions League 2026 Men's World Cup F1 Tennis FA Cup Cricket La Liga Europa League Women's Football Olympics Global Sports Cycling Sailing Sports Business Boxing Golf Serie A Bundesliga International Football NFL NBA MLB World Baseball Classic NHL MLS Transfer News FPL League Cup Championship League One League Two Women's Euros NWSL College Sports NCAAF NCAAM Peak MMA Motorsports NASCAR Culture Memorabilia & Collectibles Gaming Sports Betting Log In Subscribe Now Tickets by Viagogo Connections: Sports Edition REGION U.S. International Search Top News Podcasts NCAAM Bracket Scores & Schedule Teams Alabama Arizona Arkansas Duke Florida Gonzaga Houston Illinois Iowa State Kansas Louisville Miami (FL) Michigan State Michigan Nebraska North Carolina Purdue Saint Mary's St. John's Tennessee Texas Tech UConn Vanderbilt Virginia Wisconsin Standings Beat Our Expert's Bracket Tournament Projections Printable Bracket Predictions Game Men's March Madness LIVE First Four Updates 4m ago Tournament Forecast Challenge Our Experts The Perfect Bracket Are Cinderellas Doomed? Bracket Busters NBA Prospects to Watch Key March Madness injuries to know while filling out your men’s NCAA Tournament bracket Patrick Ngongba II and Duke are the overall No. 1 seed, but a couple of injuries could make it more vulnerable. Grant Halverson / Getty Images By Jim Root March 17, 2026 Updated 3:19 pm EDT Share full article 3 The Athletic has live coverage of the First Four from 2026 Men’s March Madness. Roster attrition is a factor during the postseason in any sport. Optimistically, college basketball’s shorter regular season should mean the title picture is less altered by injuries than, say, the NBA. That is not necessarily the case this season, though. The injury bug has recently bitten two No. 1 seeds (Duke and Michigan), and a third (Arizona) played through a flurry of nagging injuries late in the season. Multiple All-Americans on dark-horse title contenders are out for the season. Advertisement Below is a repository of all the biggest injuries impacting the men’s NCAA Tournament. We have split them into two categories: Injuries worth monitoring (the players could or will return at some point during the tournament) and out for the season. Injuries to monitor Caleb Foster and Patrick Ngongba II, Duke Both Duke starters missed the ACC tournament. The Blue Devils managed to win that event regardless, but they might need some reinforcements to handle the competition they will see if they reach the Elite Eight and beyond. Ngongba, out with a foot injury, is arguably the more valuable piece due to his defensive presence, elite finishing and sneaky-strong passing. Fortunately for the Blue Devils

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: e8-south-1
Current game label: South Regional Final
Round: Elite 8
Resolved teams: Florida (florida) vs Houston (houston)
Prior committed picks: 62
